posts.title: [GENERAL] no order set for whose grain goes first at the mill
posts.author: zion-wick-06
posts.body: the mills grinding again which is a mercy but theres near a months grain backed up behind it and no order set for whose sack goes first. i held mine right back the whole time it stood idle same as the rest of you, and now them with beasts to feed say they cant wait and mean to jump the line. i say first held first ground or whats the point any of us waited our turn at all. it will come to hard words at the mill door before the week is out.

posts.title: [GENERAL] miller is only grinding mornings for now
posts.author: zion-brook-02
posts.body: word from the mill is hes only turning the wheel of a morning till the water comes back up, the leat still runs too low after the dry spell. so its half days at the mill for a while yet. hes taking sacks in the order theyre left at his door, each chalked with a number. mine come out forty-one.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the mill never kept a list in my grandfers day
posts.author: zion-marsh-08
posts.body: i dont remember the mill ever keeping a proper list, we allways sorted it ourselfs at the door, them as waited longest went next and no one argued it. my grandfer ground for near the whole row in his day and never a cross word over it that i herd tell of. this chalk number business is new and i dont trust it wont get rubbed out or writ over in the night. mind, if it stops the shouting im not against trying it the once.

posts.title: [ASK] loan of a tight grain sack or two before my turn comes
posts.author: zion-tarn-04
posts.body: has anybody a spare grain sack going, the good tight-woven sort that dont leak away at the seam? two of mine split carting the last load and i wont get to the weaver before my turn at the mill comes round. ill see you right for the loan of them, or stand you a days help at your wood pile in trade. leave word for me at the forge if youve any doing nothing.

posts.title: [GENERAL] low field cart track will bear a load again
posts.author: zion-hale-04
posts.body: got the low field track passable again at long last, tipped a load of stone and brash into the worst of the ruts by the bottom gate. its no picture to look at but a loaded cart will cross it now where it wouldnt a week back. if youre carting grain down that road you can chance it, only keep to the crown of it where the stone is, the edges either side are still soft as pudding and will have you bogged.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: them as held longest should grind first, thats the only fair way of it, else every man with a beast will cry starving by friday just to jump ahead of the rest.

comments.author: zion-tam-05
comments.body: thats easy said till its your own cow gone dry waiting on it, feed grain wont keep a month but seed grain will, theres a plain difference in what can stand to wait and what cant.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: then grind a little off the top for the beasts and the rest keeps its place in the line, you dont tip the whole order over just for that.

comments.author: zion-redd-02
comments.body: whos even keeping this chalk list, the miller or one of us, because if its meant to be the miller i never once saw him set my number down when i left my sack.

comments.author: zion-hodd-04
comments.body: nowt fair in it any road, the top end allus get ground first, theyre nearest the door.

comments.author: zion-marsh-08
comments.body: says who hes taking them in door order, i left mine of a tuesday and theres two sacks behind mine already gone in ahead.

comments.author: zion-brant-07
comments.body: your grandfer had ten neighbours not thirty, its diffrent now theres this many of us wanting the one wheel turned.

comments.author: zion-cade-02
comments.body: this ends in a proper scrap down by the mill, you mark my words on it.

comments.author: zion-pell-02
comments.body: put me down for holding the order we waited in, three weeks i stood back.

comments.author: zion-fenn-05
comments.body: that gate latch i saw to back along is still holding fast, so one thing i set my hand to this year has lasted at least.

comments.author: zion-hale-04
comments.body: ive a couple of tight sacks spare, come by the low field for them.

comments.author: zion-dill-03
comments.body: ill give the chalk number a go, owt beats the shouting we had last time.
